data.description:
Ethereum is down $164.61 today or 3.66% to $4338.00

  • Largest percentage decrease since Oct. 7, 2025, when it dropped 3.79%
  • Down three consecutive days, down 7.57% over this period
  • Worst three day stretch since the three days ending Aug. 25, 2025, when it fell 10.11%
  • Up 3.46% month-to-date
  • Up 29.7% year-to-date

data.title: VILLAGE SUPER MARKET INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Village Super Market Inc., a prominent player in the supermarket industry, has released its annual 10-K report, detailing its financial and operational performance for the fiscal year. The report provides insights into the company's revenue growth, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in a highly competitive market.

Financial Highlights

Sales: $2,320.69 million, reflecting an increase due to a 2.1% rise in same store sales and the opening of replacement stores.

Gross Profit: $662.97 million, with a slight decrease in gross profit percentage to 28.57% due to an unfavorable change in product mix and higher promotional spending.

Operating Income: $72.07 million, an improvement driven by lower operating and administrative expenses as a percentage of sales.

Net Income: $56.38 million, up from the previous year, primarily due to increased same store sales and improved operating margins.

Net Income Per Share: Class A Basic $4.24, Class A Diluted $3.81, Class B Basic and Diluted $2.75, reflecting the overall increase in net income.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company's sales are divided into several product categories: Center Store (59.7%), Fresh (35.9%), Pharmacy (4.0%), and Other (0.4%). This breakdown highlights the company's focus on grocery, dairy, frozen, health and beauty care, general merchandise, liquor, produce, meat, deli, seafood, bakery, prepared foods, and floral products.

Geographical Performance: Village Super Market operates 34 supermarkets in New Jersey, New York, Maryland, and Pennsylvania under the ShopRite and Fairway banners, and three Gourmet Garage specialty markets in New York City. The company continues to expand its presence in these regions, with recent store openings in Watchung and Old Bridge, NJ.

Sales Units: During fiscal 2025, sales per store were $60,591, and sales per average square foot of selling space were $1,524. This indicates a strong performance in terms of sales efficiency across the company's store network.

New Production Launches: The company opened a 72,000 sq. ft. replacement ShopRite store in Watchung, NJ, and an 83,000 sq. ft. replacement ShopRite store in Old Bridge, NJ, enhancing its retail footprint and capacity.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue its expansion and remodeling program, with a budget of $75,000 for capital expenditures in fiscal 2026. This includes the construction of a replacement store in East Orange, NJ, expected to open in fiscal 2026, and another replacement store expected to open in fiscal 2027. The company anticipates same store sales growth of 1.0% to 3.0% in fiscal 2026.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: Village Super Market, Inc. has been actively involved in upgrading and expanding its supermarket chain, including store remodels and the opening of replacement stores in Watchung, NJ, and Old Bridge, NJ. The company is also investing in technology, equipment, and facility upgrades to enhance operational efficiency. Additionally, Village has invested in a real estate partnership for the development of a retail center in Old Bridge, New Jersey.

Capital Management: The company has a credit facility with Wells Fargo, which includes an unsecured revolving line of credit of $75 million and several term loans with varying interest rates and maturities. Village has been managing its debt by making principal payments and utilizing interest rate swaps to hedge against interest rate fluctuations. The company paid cash dividends of $13,308 in fiscal 2025 and has a share repurchase program with $1,349 remaining. Village also participates in a New Markets Tax Credit program, which was completed in January 2025.

Future Outlook: Village plans to invest $75 million in capital expenditures in fiscal 2026, focusing on the construction of a replacement store in East Orange, NJ, and other store remodels and upgrades. The company expects same store sales to increase by 1.0% to 3.0% in fiscal 2026 and intends to continue paying quarterly dividends at the current rate. Village believes its cash and cash equivalents, operating cash flow, and credit facility will be sufficient to meet its liquidity needs for the foreseeable future.

Challenges and Risks

Competitive Landscape: The supermarket industry is highly competitive and characterized by narrow profit margins. Village competes with various retail formats, including national, regional, and local supermarket chains, warehouse clubs, supercenters, drug stores, and online platforms. Some competitors have greater financial resources, lower merchandise acquisition costs, and lower operating expenses, which could impact Village's market position.

Operational Risks: The company faces several operational risks, including the potential impact of economic downturns in its primary operating states (New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, and Maryland), which could be exacerbated by inflation, deflation, interest rate fluctuations, and other economic factors. Additionally, disruptions to supply chains, labor shortages, and changes in consumer confidence regarding food safety could adversely affect operations.

Reliance on Wakefern: The company's reliance on Wakefern for merchandise and support services presents a significant risk. Any material change in Wakefern's operations or a modification of the relationship could increase costs and impact business operations. The failure of any Wakefern member to meet obligations could also result in increased costs for Village.

Labor Relations: Approximately 91% of Village's employees are covered by collective bargaining agreements, posing a risk of work stoppages that could impact financial results. Rising health care and pension costs under these agreements could also increase operating expenses.

Information Security: The company is exposed to risks related to information system security. Unauthorized access to customer, vendor, and associate data could damage Village's reputation and result in significant costs, including potential litigation and regulatory actions.

Market Risks: The company is subject to market risks, including interest rate fluctuations that could affect the cost of borrowing and the return on investments. The company has various term loans with interest rates tied to the Secured Overnight Financing Rate (SOFR), which could impact financial results if rates change significantly.

SEC Filing: VILLAGE SUPER MARKET INC [ VLGEA ] - 10-K - Oct. 09, 2025

VIQ Solutions Announces TSXV Listing and Voluntary Delisting from TSX
data.description:
Mississauga, Ontario--(Newsfile Corp. - October 9, 2025) - VIQ Solutions Inc.  ("VIQ", "VIQ Solutions" or the "Company"), a global provider of secure, AI-driven, digital voice and video capture technology and transcription services, is pleased to announce it has received conditional approval to list its common shares on the TSX Venture Exchange (the "TSXV"). In connection with the TSXV listing, the Company will voluntarily delist its common shares from the Toronto Stock Exchange (the "TSX").

The Company's management and Board of Directors have determined that this transition is in the best interests of VIQ. In arriving at this determination, the Company considered, among other things, the costs associated with a TSX listing versus a TSXV listing, its current market capitalization, the rules related to private placements and other forms of financing available to TSXV-listed issuers and the general suitability of a TSX listing versus a TSXV listing.

The Company expects to delist its common shares from the TSX on or about October 20, 2025 and list its common shares on the TSXV on or about October 21, 2025. The Company will remain a "reporting issuer" under applicable Canadian securities laws through the listing transition process. The Company will retain the trading symbol "VQS" once listed on the TSXV. Shareholder approval is not required under the policies of the TSX to proceed with the transition as the TSXV is an acceptable alternative market. No action is required by shareholders in connection with the transition of the Company's listing to the TSXV.

For more information about VIQ, please visit viqsolutions.com.

About VIQ Solutions

VIQ Solutions is a global provider of secure, AI-driven, digital voice and video capture technology and transcription services. VIQ offers a seamless, comprehensive solution suite that delivers intelligent automation, enhanced with human review, to drive transformation in the way content is captured, secured, and repurposed into actionable information. The cyber-secure, AI technology and services platform are implemented in the most rigid security environments including criminal justice, legal, insurance, government, corporate finance, media, and transcription service provider markets, enabling them to improve the quality and accessibility of evidence, to easily identify predictive insights and to achieve digital transformation faster and at a lower cost.

Forward-Looking Statements

Certain statements included in this press release constitute forward-looking statements or forward-looking information (collectively, "forward-looking statements") under applicable securities legislation. Such forward-looking statements or information are provided for the purpose of providing information about management's current expectations and plans relating to the future. Readers are cautioned that reliance on such information may not be appropriate for other purposes.

Forward-looking statements typically contain statements with words such as "anticipate", "believe", "expect", "plan", "intend", "estimate", "propose", "project" or similar words, including negatives thereof, and suggest future outcomes or that certain events or conditions "may" or "will" occur. These statements are only predictions. Forward-looking statements in this press release include but are not limited to statements with respect to the proposed timing of the delisting and listing.

Forward-looking statements are based on several factors and assumptions which have been used to develop such statements, but which may prove to be incorrect. Although VIQ believes that the expectations reflected in such forward-looking statements are reasonable, undue reliance should not be placed on forward-looking statements because VIQ can give no assurance that such expectations will prove to be correct.

Forward-looking statements are necessarily based on a number of opinions, assumptions and estimates that while considered reasonable by the Company as of the date of this press release, are subject to known and unknown risks, uncertainties, assumptions, and other factors that may cause the actual results, level of activity, performance or achievements to be materially different from those expressed or implied by such forward-looking statements, including but not limited to the factors described in greater detail in the "Risk Factors" section of the Company's annual information form and in the Company's other materials filed on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca.

These factors are not intended to represent a complete list of the factors that could affect the Company; however, these factors should be considered carefully. Such estimates and assumptions may prove to be incorrect or overstated. The forward-looking statements contained in this press release are made as of the date of this press release and the Company expressly disclaims any obligations to update or alter such statements, or the factors or assumptions underlying them, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise, except as required by law.

Contact:

Jacob Manning VIQ Solutions

Email: marketing@viqsolutions.com

data.title: Levi Strauss falls after forecasting annual profit below estimates
data.description:
** Denim-maker Levi Strauss' shares fall ~6% to $23.09 in after-market trading

** LEVI raises annual profit forecast banking on strong demand for wide-leg denim bottoms in Europe and the Americas despite higher tariffs, but the projection fell short of Wall Street expectations

** Projects annual adj. EPS in the range of $1.27 to $1.32, up from prior forecast of $1.25 to $1.30 per share

** However, range mid-point came below analysts' estimate of $1.31, according to data compiled by LSEG

** LEVI expects FY 2025 organic net revenue from continuing operations, excluding Dockers, to rise about 6%, vs prior target of a 4.5% to 5.5% increase

** Q3 adj. EPS came in at 34 cents, from 33 cents a year ago

** Q3 net revenue rose 7% YoY to $1.54 bln, above avg estimate of $1.50 bln

** As of Thursday's close, stock had risen 41.8% YTD

data.title: PTC Therapeutics Executive Sells Shares
data.description:

RelationshipEXEC. VP AND CLO, 10% Owner
TypeSell

Mark Elliott Boulding, Executive VP and CLO of PTC Therapeutics, and a 10% owner, sold 3,375 shares of common stock on October 7, 2025. data.title: North Valley Resources Closes Non-Brokered Private Placement
data.description:
Kamloops, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - October 9, 2025) - North Valley Resources Ltd. ("the Company") is pleased to announce that, further to its news release of September 15, 2025, the Company has closed a non-brokered private placement financing of 7,500,000 common shares of the Company ("Common Shares") at a price of $0.02 per Common Share for gross proceeds of $150,000 (the "Private Placement").

No commissions or fees were paid in connection with the Private Placement. The net proceeds of the Private Placement will be used for general working capital requirements. In accordance with the policies of the CSE, shareholders of more than 50% of the Common Shares approved the Private Placement by a written resolution prior to closing.

All securities issued pursuant to the Private Placement are subject to a statutory hold period of four months plus one day from the date of closing.

About North Valley Resources

The Company is primarily focused on exploring its optioned Comstock Property covering 4,462 hectares. The Comstock Copper-Gold property is located adjacent to the Coquihalla highway, approximately 10 kms south of Merritt, B.C.

On behalf of the Company

Quinn Ellerbeck, Chief Financial Officer & Director

For further information, please contact Quinn Ellerbeck at quinn@lacombeventures.com.

Forward-Looking Statements: This press release may contain "forward-looking information or statements" within the meaning of Canadian securities laws, which may include, but are not limited to statements relating to its future business plans. data.title: Africa Energy Announces Block 11B/12B Operations Update
data.description:
Africa Energy

VANCOUVER, BC, Oct. 9, 2025 /CNW/ – Africa Energy Corp. (TSX Venture: AFE) (Nasdaq First North: AEC) (“Africa Energy” or the “Company”) announces that the Company, through its investment in Main Street 1549 Pty Ltd. (“Main Street”), which holds an interest in Block 11B/12B, offshore the Republic of South Africa, has obtained an extension to the deadline for the submission of a new Environmental and Social Impact Assessment ("ESIA") to May 4, 2026.

The extension to the ESIA has been granted in light of the recent decision by the Western Cape High Court in South Africa ("High Court") to set aside an environmental authorization for offshore exploration operations in Block 5/6/7 (held by an unrelated party) so that additional, new and amended environmental assessments can be conducted and placed before the Minister of Mineral and Petroleum Resources for reconsideration. An application for leave to appeal this decision to the Supreme Court of Appeal has been launched by the unrelated party. The Company is therefore engaging with its advisors, including legal counsel, to determine the amendments and additions which may be required to its ESIA as a result of the High Court decision. Due to the High Court decision, which is pending appeal to the Supreme Court of Appeal, there is no certainty on the timing of the grant of the environmental authorization after the submission of the amended ESIA. The grant of the environmental authorization is a pre-requisite to the grant of the production right in relation to Block 11B/12B. The Company is continuing to gather information and work with applicable regulators in order to better understand the current situation. Africa Energy will provide an update on such timing and status once it has further information.

Main Street currently holds a 10% participating interest in Block 11B/12B, offshore South Africa. Subject to all relevant regulatory approvals by South African authorities in respect to the withdrawal of the joint venture partners in Block 11B/12B and completion of the restructuring of Main Street (previously announced May 29, 2025), the Company expects to hold a 75% direct interest in Block 11B/12B. Both the assignment of the withdrawing parties’ interest in Block 11B/12B to Main Street and the completion of the Main Street restructuring require grant of the production right in relation to Block 11B/12B.

Important information

This is information that Africa Energy is obliged to make public pursuant to the EU Market Abuse Regulation. The information was submitted for publication through the agency of the contact persons set out above on October 9, 2025, at 5:30 p.m. ET.

The Company’s certified advisor on Nasdaq First North Growth Market is Bergs Securities AB, +46 739 49 62 50, rutger.ahlerup@bergssecurities.se.

Forward looking statements

Certain statements contained in this press release constitute forward-looking information. These statements relate to future events or the Company’s future performance, business prospects and opportunities, which are based on assumptions of management. There is no certainty that the Parties will satisfy all the conditions in the Agreements and execute the assignment agreement and that, if executed, the terms in the assignment agreement will be consistent with the terms of the Agreements. Further, there is no certainty that the Parties will obtain all the required regulatory approvals necessary to effect the transfer of the participating interests.

The use of any of the words “will”, “expected”, “planned”, “intends” and similar expressions and statements relating to matters that are not historical facts are intended to identify forward-looking information and are based on the Company’s current belief or assumptions as to the outcome and timing of certain future events. These forward-looking statements involve risks and uncertainties relating to, among other things, changes in oil prices, results of exploration and development activities, including results, timing and costs of exploration and development activity in the Company’s area of operations and, uninsured risks, regulatory changes, defects in title, availability of funds required to participate in the exploration and development activities, or of financing on reasonable terms, availability of materials and equipment on satisfactory terms, outcome of commercial negotiations with government and other regulatory authorities, timeliness of government or other regulatory approvals, actual performance of facilities, availability of third party service providers, equipment and processes relative to specifications and expectations and unanticipated environmental impacts on operations. Actual future results may differ materially. Various assumptions or factors are typically applied in drawing conclusions or making the forecasts or projections set out in forward-looking information. Those assumptions and factors are based on information currently available to the Company. The forward-looking information contained in this release is made as of the date hereof and the Company is not obligated to update or revise any forward-looking information, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise, except as required by applicable securities laws. Because of the risks, uncertainties and assumptions contained herein, investors should not place undue reliance on forward-looking information. The foregoing statements expressly qualify any forward-looking information.

Neither TSX Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

View PDF version

For further information, please contact:

Jeromie Kufflick

Chief Financial Officer

+1 (587) 333-6489

info@africaenergycorp.com

www.africaenergycorp.com

About Africa Energy Corp.

Africa Energy Corp. is a Canadian oil and gas exploration company focused on South Africa. The Company is listed in Toronto on TSX Venture Exchange (ticker “AFE”) and in Stockholm on Nasdaq First North Growth Market (ticker “AEC”).

data.title: Industrials Down on Shutdown Fears - Industrials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of industrial and transportation companies fell sharply amid fears that a government shutdown would weigh on U.S. economic activity.

A pair of Wall Street firms estimate that initial jobless claims rose slightly to 224,000 in the week ended Sept. 27 from 218,000 in the prior week, as the labor market appeared to hold up ahead of the recently commenced government shutdown.

GM is bringing back its Chevrolet Bolt with a new model that it expects to account for much of its electric-vehicle volume next year.

Italian sports-car maker Ferrari's shares slumped after new long-term profitability targets missed expectations.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Health Care Down as Deal Activity Offset by Regulatory Fears - Health Care Roundup
data.description:
Health-care companies ticked down as deal activity was offset by regulatory fears.

Danish obesity-drug maker Novo Nordisk agreed to acquire Akero Therapeutics for up to $5.2 billion in cash.

Shares of Akero rallied, while Novo Nordisk retreated.

Pfizer shares continued a recent retreat during which the Big Pharma company has given back a large chunk of the gains associated with its compromise on tariffs.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Financials Down as First Brands' Worries Linger - Financials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of banks and other financial institutions declined amid fears that a messy auto-parts bankruptcy could point to broader issue in credit markets.

BlackRock reportedly requested cash withdrawal from a Jefferies Financial fund exposed to the First Brands' bankruptcy. Shares of Jefferies had plunged Wednesday, and compounded those losses with another slip Thursday.

Raistone, a trade-finance firm also caught up in the bankruptcy, requested an outside examination of its collapse and how $2.3 billion in assets "simply vanished."

HSBC Holdings made a $13.6 billion offer to buy out other shareholders in Hang Seng Bank, a smaller Hong Kong lender that it has long controlled, as the global lender turns its focus to its domestic market.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Consumer Cos Down Despite Pepsi, Delta Strength - Consumer Roundup
data.description:
Consumer companies slipped as a vacuum of economic data offset generally strong earnings.

Delta Air Lines rallied after the Atlanta carrier said a recent resurgence in demand for tickets is set to continue, and highlighted the success of a strategy to sell more premium seating.

PepsiCo tapped a Walmart executive, Steve Schmitt, to fill its top finance role as the packaged-food company aims to turn around its slowing soda and snack sales following modest third-quarter revenue growth.

Japanese convenience-store chain 7-Eleven's same-store retail fuel volume declined by 4.8% year to year in the fiscal second quarter.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Tilray Brands, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Tilray Brands, Inc., a leading global cannabis-lifestyle and consumer packaged goods company, has released its latest Form 10-Q report, showcasing significant improvements in both financial and operational performance. The report highlights the company's strategic initiatives and market expansion efforts, reflecting its commitment to growth and profitability in the competitive cannabis and beverage industries.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Revenue: $209.5 million, reflecting a 5% increase from the prior year period, driven by growth in cannabis and distribution segments.
  • Gross Profit: $57.5 million, a decrease of 4% compared to the previous year, primarily due to increased cost of goods sold.
  • Operating Income: $2.1 million, a significant improvement from an operating loss of $36.6 million in the prior year, attributed to reduced operating expenses.
  • Net Income: $1.5 million, compared to a net loss of $34.7 million in the previous year, indicating a turnaround in profitability.
  • Net Loss Per Share - Basic and Diluted: $(0.00), showing an improvement from $(0.04) in the prior year, reflecting better financial performance.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company reports revenue across four segments: Beverage, Cannabis, Distribution, and Wellness. The Cannabis segment saw net revenue of $64.5 million, driven by Canadian adult-use cannabis and international cannabis sales. The Beverage segment reported net revenue of $55.7 million, with a focus on core brands and innovation initiatives. Distribution revenue was $74 million, while Wellness revenue reached $15.2 million.
  • Geographical Performance: The company operates in multiple regions, with significant contributions from the USA ($64 million), Canada ($58.2 million), and EMEA ($85.3 million). The company continues to lead the Canadian cannabis market with a slight increase in market share to 9.4%.
  • New Product Launches: The company has launched a lineup of Hemp Derived Delta-9 products, Non-Alcoholic beverages, and clean label Energy drinks, reflecting a focus on innovation and portfolio diversification.
  • Beverage Market Trends: The company is enhancing its relevance in home markets through strategic portfolio optimization and distribution network refinement. Recent product resets have improved distribution for core brands like Shock Top and SweetWater Brewing.
  • Canadian Cannabis Market Trends: The company continues to lead the Canadian market, with a focus on enhancing its global supply chain and cultivation footprint to support growing demand. Price compression remains a challenge due to competition among Licensed Producers.
  • International Cannabis Trends: The company is well-positioned in Europe, particularly in Germany, where it is one of three manufacturers of medical cannabis. The company is expanding its presence in Poland, the UK, Ireland, Italy, and Australia, leveraging its vertically-integrated infrastructure.
  • Beverage Segment Project 420: The company is implementing Project 420 to streamline its beverage operations, focusing on SKU rationalization, geographic rationalization, distributor rationalization, and synergy optimization. The project aims to improve profitability and growth for acquired brands.
  • Future Outlook: The company is focused on leveraging its brands and infrastructure to drive revenue growth and achieve industry-leading profitability. It plans to continue expanding its business through organic growth and strategic acquisitions, while managing its cost structure to maintain a strong financial position.

SEC Filing: Tilray Brands, Inc. [ TLRY ] - 10-Q - Oct. 09, 2025

data.title: Australia shares set to fall at open, NZ down
data.description:
Australian shares are set to open lower on Friday, with possible drags from gold and energy stocks as commodity prices eased, while investors awaited a slew of corporate results due later in the month.

The local share price index futures (YAPcm1) fell 0.4%, a 12.8-point discount to the underlying S&P/ASX 200 index close. The benchmark rose 0.3% on Thursday.

New Zealand's benchmark S&P/NZX 50 index fell 0.1% to 13,554.76 in early trade.

data.title: Utilities Down, But not by Much, on Defensive Demand - Utilities Roundup
data.description:
Shares of power producers fell, but not by as much as the broad market, as traders continued to seek out safe havens amid political turmoil in several major economies.

Danish wind-power developer Orsted will slash its workforce by 25% over the next couple of years as it scales back its global footprint to focus more on selected offshore and European markets.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Tech Down as AI Frenzy Cools - Tech Roundup
data.description:
Shares of technology companies slipped as the artificial-intelligence frenzy cooled off.

The Roundhill Magnificent Seven exchange-traded fund, which tracks many of the big players in AI, fell, but remain roughly 37% higher over the last 12 months.

Investors are concerned that capital expenditure on AI infrastructure has become a reckless arms race, with apparently "circular" cross-investments between chip makers, data-center builders and Big Tech software makers muddying the demand picture.

Alphabet's Google will pump about $5.82 billion into Brussels, the capital of capital into Belgium over the next two years, the latest large-scale investment from a U.S. tech giant bolstering its data-center infrastructure in Europe.

Microsoft recently unveiled similar multibillion-dollar AI investment plans in the United Kingdom.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Communications Services Down on Deal Skepticism - Communications Services Roundup
data.description:
Communications-services companies fell amid skepticism about deal activity. Shares of Paramount Skydance and Warner Bros.

Discovery fell following reports that Paramount Chief Executive David Ellison and his father, Oracle co-founder Larry Ellison, are lining up private-equity backers for a bid that could run as high as $60 billion.
